Redirect unscoped WP URLs back to scoped WP URLs in service worker (#3370)

## Summary
- When the service worker intercepts a request from a scoped page
(identified via the Referer header) but the request URL itself is
unscoped, redirect it to the scoped version of the URL instead of
directly handling it as a PHP request. This prevents scope loss when
navigating around a scoped WordPress instance.
- Skip intercepting cross-origin requests initiated from scoped pages,
letting them pass through to the network instead.

## Context

Previously, when a scoped WordPress page linked to an unscoped URL (e.g.
a WordPress-generated link without the scope prefix), the service worker
would directly handle it via `handleScopedRequest()`. This worked for
that single request, but the resulting page would be loaded at an
unscoped URL. Navigating from that unscoped page to another unscoped URL
resulted in losing the scope entirely, and the service worker would no
longer be able to match requests to the correct WordPress instance.

By redirecting to the scoped URL instead, the browser's address bar and
subsequent navigations all stay within the scope.

## Test plan
- [ ] Load a scoped WordPress Playground instance
- [ ] Navigate to a WordPress link that doesn't include the scope prefix
- [ ] Verify the browser redirects to the scoped version of the URL
- [ ] Verify subsequent navigation continues to work within the scope
- [ ] Verify cross-origin requests from scoped pages are not intercepted

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(api): improve rate limit headers and error metadata

getRateLimitHeaders included Retry-After on every response, violating
RFC 6585 which reserves it for 429/503. RateLimitError only carried
retryAfter but the class supports limit/remaining/reset fields.

Changes:
- Remove Retry-After from getRateLimitHeaders (X-RateLimit-* only)
- Include limit, remaining, and reset in RateLimitError on rejection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(hooks): centralize stream cleanup in useRetryGeneration

Early returns on terminal SSE events (complete, error, cancelled) did
not cancel the reader or abort the controller, causing resource leaks.
Wrapping the reader loop in try/finally ensures cleanup on every exit
path.

Changes:
- Wrap reader loop and buffer-flush in inner try/finally block
- Call reader.cancel() and controller.abort() in finally clause

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): bind OAuth state token redemption to provider

The state redemption was not bound to the issuing provider, allowing a
token issued for one provider to be redeemed against another. consume()
now requires and verifies a provider parameter, preventing cross-provider
token replay attacks.

Changes:
- Add provider parameter to OAuthStateStore.consume interface
- Add eq(provider) to the WHERE clause in consume implementation
- Update validateOAuthStateToken to require and forward provider
- Pass google_calendar provider in Google OAuth callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(logging): align correlation ID header with proxy

The proxy sets x-correlation-id on every request, but REQUEST_ID_HEADER
in request-context.ts was x-request-id. createRequestContext therefore
never found the correlation ID the proxy set, generating a fresh UUID
and breaking request correlation across the stack.

Changes:
- Change REQUEST_ID_HEADER from x-request-id to x-correlation-id
- attachRequestIdHeader automatically picks up the new value

commit.message:
chore(docker): Refactor sandbox state management and improve Docker integration (#1068)

* Refactor sandbox state management and improve Docker integration

- Removed FileSandboxStateStore and SandboxStateStore classes for a cleaner architecture.
- Enhanced LocalContainerBackend to handle port allocation retries and introduced environment variable support for sandbox host configuration.
- Updated Paths class to include host_base_dir for Docker volume mounts and ensured proper permissions for sandbox directories.
- Modified ExtensionsConfig to improve error handling when loading configuration files and adjusted environment variable resolution.
- Updated sandbox configuration to include a replicas option for managing concurrent sandbox containers.
- Improved logging and context management in SandboxMiddleware for better sandbox lifecycle handling.
- Enhanced network port allocation logic to bind to 0.0.0.0 for compatibility with Docker.
- Updated Docker Compose files to ensure proper volume management and environment variable configuration.
- Created scripts to ensure necessary configuration files are present before starting services.
- Cleaned up unused MCP server configurations in extensions_config.example.json.

commit.message:
feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

* feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

Implements M2 (cryptd-7uw): the minimal slice that proves the full
L1 architecture is wired up and runnable end-to-end without LLM.

New packages:
- internal/engine: deterministic rules engine (NewGame, Move, Look)
  with typed errors NoExitError and LockedError
- internal/interpreter: RulesInterpreter maps text commands to
  EngineActions (go <dir>, look, quit and aliases)
- internal/narrator: TemplateNarrator produces fixed-template
  narrations for all event types
- internal/renderer: CLIRenderer reads stdin / writes stdout
- internal/game: Loop wires engine+interpreter+narrator+renderer;
  drives full pipeline until quit or context cancel

CLI:
- crypt headless --scenario <id>: runs headless mode; respects
  CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var for scenario lookup path

Tests:
- Unit tests in all packages (no build tag)
- Integration tests in internal/game with adversarial fakes covering
  ctx cancel, channel close, quit event, narrate/render error paths
- E2E test (build tag e2e) compiles binary and runs scripted session
- Coverage ≥ 90% on all M2 packages (engine 93.8%, game 92.1%,
  narrator/renderer 100%, interpreter 95%)

Fixtures:
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: M2 acceptance script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review — state corruption, quit narration, context, types

Addresses all issues surfaced by Cursor Bugbot review:

- engine: validate destination room before mutating GameState so a
  failed Move never leaves state corrupted (state-before-error-check)
- engine: hidden connections now return NoExitError (undiscoverable
  until revealed) instead of LockedError
- engine/doc.go: remove premature 'combat, inventory' claims
- game/loop: route renderer-emitted quit events through dispatch so
  quit narration ('Farewell, adventurer.') is rendered before exit
- game/loop: render quit narration after dispatch returns quit event
- game/loop: map LockedError -> 'locked_door' event, NoExitError ->
  'no_exit' event so narrators can give specific feedback
- narrator: add 'locked_door' and 'no_exit' template narrations
- renderer/cli: move stdin scan into goroutine (startOnce) so
  Render never blocks on input and ctx cancellation is honoured
- cmd/crypt: use filepath.Join for cross-platform path assembly
- cmd/crypt: rename usage strings from 'crypt' to 'cryptd'
- e2e: build binary as 'cryptd' matching documented binary name
- testdata/scripts: fix header comment (no --script flag exists)
- tests: remove unused errRenderer; add locked_door/no_exit/unknown
  dispatch coverage; use select+timeout in renderer tests

Coverage: engine 91.2%, game 97.6%, narrator/renderer 100% (total 91.6%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): hidden connections not leaked in exitList

exitList filtered over all room.Connections without checking type,
causing hidden connections to appear in LookResult.Exits and
MoveResult.Exits — contradicting the engine's own policy that hidden
connections are undiscoverable until revealed.

Now exitList skips entries where conn.Type == "hidden", consistent
with Move which already returns NoExitError for hidden directions.

Added tests:
- TestLook_HiddenConnectionsNotInExits: verifies north (hidden) absent
- TestMove_HiddenIsNoExit: verifies hidden direction returns NoExitError
Updated minimal.yaml: added secret_room with hidden north exit from entrance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(game): propagate internal engine errors from dispatch instead of swallowing them as no_exit

NoExitError and LockedError are expected game conditions mapped to distinct
events. Any other error from Engine.Move indicates state/scenario corruption
and must propagate so callers can surface a diagnostic.

Added TestLoop_MoveInternalErrorPropagates to cover the new branch.

commit.message:
fix: detect tmux session for raw terminals that typed 'tmux attach'

When a raw terminal session runs 'tmux attach' inside it, the
session has no tmuxSessionName but has a tmux client process in
its tree. Now detects this by:
1. Getting all tmux client PIDs via tmux list-clients
2. Checking if any client PID is a descendant of the PTY
3. If found, resolves the tmux session name and looks up pane
   PID/CWD from that session

Also added getDescendantPids() using BFS over ps -eo pid=,ppid=
for reliable descendant detection on macOS.

feat: Plan 034 — plugin session matching by process tree + global store

Complete rewrite of CopilotCollector matching logic:

1. Walk terminal's process tree (via tmux pane PID for tmux sessions)
2. If no 'copilot' process in tree → return null (no data for this pane)
3. Use pane CWD to query global session-store.db for session UUID
4. Read session-state/{uuid}/session.db for todos/phases/context
5. Clear UI when copilot exits (send null plugin_data)

Key changes:
- CopilotCollector.collectForSession() now accepts processes[] param
- MessageRouter resolves tmux pane PID (not PTY PID) for tree walking
- Global session-store.db used for CWD→UUID lookup (not scanning all DBs)
- IDataCollector interface updated with optional processes param
- Null data sent to clear pane UI when copilot no longer in tree

Result: plugin data only shows in panes running copilot.
Non-copilot panes show nothing. Different copilot sessions
show different data per pane.

All 25 tests pass. Build clean.

commit.message:
docs: Plan 034 architecture — process tree + global store matching

Simple mode plan (CS-2, single phase, 7 tasks).

Matching algorithm:
1. Walk terminal's process tree to find copilot process
2. If no copilot → skip (no plugin data for this pane)
3. Get tmux pane CWD via tmux list-panes
4. Query global session-store.db (sessions table) by CWD
5. Read matching session-state/{uuid}/session.db

Key research finding: lsof unreliable (copilot opens DB on demand).
Global session-store.db has CWD→UUID mapping — use that instead
of scanning all individual session DBs.

8 research findings. Lightweight testing.

commit.message:
Reduce and gate kinm trace volume (#15)

* Reduce and gate kinm trace volume

Remove the noisiest OTEL instrumentation from kinm and make the
remaining spans opt-in. This drops raw SQL and argument attributes,
removes redundant adapter and DB helper spans, and replaces
high-cardinality list and watch attributes with coarse flags and
counts.

Add KINM_TRACE_LEVEL with off, basic, and verbose levels and default
it to off. Spans in this package now only start when that level
allows them and the incoming context already carries a parent span,
which keeps standalone background usage quiet while preserving HTTP
request traces when enabled.

* Update pkg/db/strategy.go

commit.message:
docs: Plan 034 spec — Plugin session matching by Process ID

Match terminal panes to Copilot session DBs by walking the process
tree to find copilot PIDs, not by CWD comparison.

Key approach:
1. Copilot CLI writes copilot_pid to session_state on startup
2. For tmux panes, use tmux list-panes to get actual shell PID
3. Walk process tree from shell PID to find copilot process
4. Match copilot PID to session DB's stored copilot_pid
5. Fallback to CWD matching if PID not found

CS-2 (small), 8 acceptance criteria, Mode: Simple.
